title: taraxacum subeximium
intro: Taraxacum subeximium, also known as the lesser hawk's beard, is a species of flowering plant in the dandelion genus. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and diuretic effects. The plant's leaves and roots have been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and acting as an antioxidant. Taraxacum subeximium can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in traditional folk remedies for its purported healing properties.
